name: Arroyo del Ojanco Olive Groves Loop
name_original: Los olivares del Arroyo del Ojanco - circular por la Sierra de Segura
description: An easy 15.3-mile mountain biking route through the olive groves and natural beauty of Sierra de Segura, taking about 1 hour 53 minutes.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Arroyo Del Ojanco are situated in the province of Jaén, serving as a gateway to the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park. The region features a rugged landscape of mountains and valleys, with the village located near the confluence of the Guadalimar, Guadalmena, and Beas rivers. Expansive olive groves characterize much of the surrounding terrain, providing a distinct backdrop for rides. This area offers diverse natural features and varied topography suitable for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Arroyo Del Ojanco The most popular mountain bike trail is Arroyo del Ojanco Olive Groves Loop, a 15.3 miles (24.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 54 minutes to complete. This easy route winds through the region's characteristic olive groves. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Eagle Rock loop from PR-A 182 Cerro del Pavo, a moderate 16.2 miles (26.1 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, offering views from higher ground. Local mountain bikers also love the Manantial del Maguillo – Cerro Peguera loop from Beas de Segura, a 25.3 miles (40.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain including a spring and a hill, often completed in about 3 hours 51 minutes. Mountain biking around Arroyo Del Ojanco is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and extensive olive groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging ascents. The routes in Arroyo Del Ojanco are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 30 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Arroyo Del Ojanco's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Arroyo del Ojanco?
A: The region around Arroyo del Ojanco is characterized by rugged mountainous terrain, deep valleys, and extensive olive groves. You'll find trails winding through these diverse landscapes, often near the confluence of the Guadalimar, Guadalmena, and Beas rivers, offering varied scenery for your ride.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Arroyo del Ojanco?
A: Yes, there are options for beginners. An easy route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993688845/arroyo-del-ojanco-olive-groves-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo del Ojanco Olive Groves Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great choice. It's about 24.7 km long and takes around 1 hour 54 minutes, winding through the region's characteristic olive groves with minimal elevation gain.

Q: What are some moderate mountain bike trails in the area?
A: For those seeking a moderate challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24523563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eagle Rock loop from PR-A 182 Cerro del Pavo&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain and scenic views over 26.1 km. Another popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39556040" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Manantial del Maguillo – Cerro Peguera loop from Beas de Segura&lt;/a&gt;, which is longer at 40.7 km and leads through varied terrain including a spring and a hill.

Q: What are the best times of year for mountain biking in Arroyo del Ojanco?
A: The Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park, where Arroyo del Ojanco is located, generally offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking during spring and autumn. These seasons provide milder temperatures, making rides more comfortable than the heat of summer or the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Arroyo del Ojanco are designed as loops. For example, the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993688845/arroyo-del-ojanco-olive-groves-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo del Ojanco Olive Groves Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24523563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eagle Rock loop from PR-A 182 Cerro del Pavo&lt;/a&gt; are both circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can expect to see expansive olive groves, mountainous vistas, and river valleys. The Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park is known for its unique biodiversity. You might also encounter local attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4376846" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo del Ojanco Village and Olive Groves&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4765482" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the wide valley&lt;/a&gt; on some routes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Arroyo del Ojanco?
A: The mountain bike routes around Arroyo del Ojanco are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Riders often appreciate the varied topography and the distinct backdrop of olive groves, making for a unique cycling experience.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many routes in the mountainous terrain offer stunning vistas. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24523563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eagle Rock loop from PR-A 182 Cerro del Pavo&lt;/a&gt; provides views from higher ground due to its significant elevation gain. You can also find specific points like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4765482" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the wide valley&lt;/a&gt; marked as highlights.

Q: What other activities are available in the Arroyo del Ojanco area besides mountain biking?
A: Beyond mountain biking, the region offers a wealth of outdoor activities. You can enjoy hiking, water sports like canoeing and kayaking on local reservoirs, fishing, and bird watching in the natural park. Cultural explorations include the traditional architecture of Arroyo del Ojanco and historical sites like the Castle of Segura de la Sierra.

Q: Are there any longer mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39556040" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Manantial del Maguillo – Cerro Peguera loop from Beas de Segura&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 40.7 km route. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40015176" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mountainbike loop from Peñolite&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 31.1 km with considerable elevation changes.

Q: Where can I find more information about the natural park surrounding Arroyo del Ojanco?
A: Arroyo del Ojanco serves as a gateway to the expansive Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park. This park is renowned for its dramatic landscapes, unique biodiversity, and a variety of natural features, including mountains, valleys, and rivers. Exploring the park offers a deeper immersion into the region's natural environment.
tips: Road between the hills in which, when leaving the main roads, you enter a beautiful wild area with perfect dirt roads for riding.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The Komoot data suggests this route takes approximately 1 hour and 53 minutes to complete. However, this can vary based on your pace, stops for scenery, and the specific trail conditions on the day.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Los olivares del Arroyo del Ojanco circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied, largely following an old railway line, which means relatively consistent gradients but also some uphill sections. You'll encounter dirt paths, gravel farm tracks, and even poorly maintained tarmac that can turn into gravel. Be prepared for potentially rough track sections and 'hell mud' in olive grove areas, especially after rain. Some parts are shared with vehicles.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginner mountain bikers?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'easy' according to Komoot. While some sections of the broader Segura Nature Trail are described as 'not difficult' due to their straightness, the varied surface conditions, including rough tracks and potential mud, mean that a basic level of mountain biking experience and comfort on varied terrain would be beneficial.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or scenic highlights along the way?
faqs.answer: A dominant feature is the 'sea of olives' with vast olive groves. The route also passes through the stunning Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park. You'll encounter pine masses, Mediterranean scrubs, and potentially glimpses of imposing mountains like La Sagra. A specific highlight is the Balsa and Olive Groves of Los Robados. The trail also utilizes old railway infrastructures like tunnels, passes, and viaducts, adding a unique historical dimension.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated for this specific route, mountain biking in the Sierra de Segura region is generally best during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder, wetter conditions, increasing the likelihood of mud, especially in olive grove areas.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Los olivares del Arroyo del Ojanco trail?
faqs.answer: Information regarding specific dog regulations for this exact route is not available. However, as it passes through the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all trail users. Always practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or is there an entrance fee for this trail or the Natural Park?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees mentioned for accessing this particular mountain bike route. As it lies within the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park, general park regulations apply, but typically there are no fees for entry to the park itself for recreational activities like mountain biking.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Los olivares del Arroyo del Ojanco circular route?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the starting point of this exact circular route is not provided. However, Arroyo del Ojanco is a town, and you would typically look for public parking areas within or on the outskirts of the town that allow access to the trail network. It's recommended to check local maps or satellite views for suitable parking spots near the trail's beginning.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route utilizes sections of several official trails. You will find it intersects with parts of the Vía Verde de Segura, the Cordel de Andalucía, and the historic Baeza-Utiel railway line.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and potential for 'hell mud' after rain, a mountain bike with wide tires is advisable. It's also crucial to carry sufficient water, as sources can be scarce along some parts of the route. Essential gear includes a helmet, repair kit, navigation tools, and appropriate clothing for the weather conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or water sources directly on the trail?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through a region with towns like Arroyo del Ojanco, specific amenities directly on the trail itself are not guaranteed. The trail research notes a scarcity of water sources along some parts of the route, so it's essential to carry enough water. For cafes or other services, you would likely need to detour into nearby towns.
